tasks.title: AI module unit tests — tokenEstimator, responseParser, aiCache, promptBuilder
tasks.description:
Add comprehensive unit tests for the AI subsystem in `apps/api/src/ai/`. These modules are core to all AI features but have zero test coverage.

**1. tokenEstimator tests** (`apps/api/src/ai/__tests__/tokenEstimator.test.ts`):
- Test `estimateTokens(text)`: empty string → 0, short text ("hello world") → ~3, long text (1000 chars) → ~250. Verify it returns a number ≥ 0.
- Test `checkPromptSize(system, user, maxOutput)`: normal case passes (short prompts, 8192 max), throws `GraphQLError` with code `PROMPT_TOO_LARGE` when prompts would exceed 200K context window. Test the 80% warning threshold by mocking the logger and verifying `log.warn` is called.

**2. responseParser tests** (`apps/api/src/ai/__tests__/responseParser.test.ts`):
- Test `stripFences(raw)`: bare JSON returns unchanged, ` ```json\n{...}\n``` ` extracts inner content, preamble text before fences ("Here are the tasks:\n```json\n[...]\n```") extracts correctly, bare JSON with trailing text extracts by bracket matching, no JSON at all returns trimmed string.
- Test `parseJSON(raw, schema)`: valid JSON matching schema returns parsed data, invalid JSON throws `GraphQLError('Failed to parse AI response')`, valid JSON not matching schema throws `GraphQLError('AI response did not match expected format')`. Use a simple `z.object({ title: z.string() })` test schema.

**3. aiCache tests** (`apps/api/src/ai/__tests__/aiCache.test.ts`):
- Import `aiCache` and `hashPrompt` from `../aiCache.js`.
- Test `hashPrompt`: same inputs produce same hash, different inputs produce different hashes.
- Test `aiCache.set/get`: set a value, get returns it, get with wrong key returns undefined.
- Test TTL expiry: set with short TTL (50ms), wait 60ms, get returns undefined. Use `vi.advanceTimersByTime()` with `vi.useFakeTimers()`.
- Test LRU eviction: fill cache beyond capacity (500 entries), verify oldest entries are evicted.

**4. promptBuilder tests** (`apps/api/src/ai/__tests__/promptBuilder.test.ts`):
- Test `buildTaskPlanPrompt`: verify it returns `{ systemPrompt, userPrompt }`, systemPrompt contains the task count cap ("5–10 tasks" and "NEVER more than 10"), userPrompt contains the project title wrapped in `<user_input>` tags.
- Test `buildProjectOptionsPrompt`: returns 2-key object, userPrompt mentions "exactly 3 distinct project interpretations".
- Test `userInput(label, value)`: wraps correctly with `<user_input label="...">...</user_input>` tags.
- Test context compression: pass a 500-char description to `buildTaskPlanPrompt`, verify userPrompt truncates it (the function uses `truncate(desc, 400)`).

All tests are pure unit tests — no database, no network. Mock the pino logger where needed (`vi.mock('../utils/logger.js')`).
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm --filter api test passes with all new tests green
pnpm typecheck passes
At least 20 test cases across the 4 test files
No database or network calls — pure unit tests only
Logger is mocked to prevent console noise during tests

tasks.title: CI test gate + task/sprint integration tests
tasks.description:
Add a test execution step to the CI pipeline and expand integration test coverage beyond auth to task and sprint CRUD.

**1. CI test gate** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`):
Currently the CI workflow runs `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build` but does NOT run tests. Add a test step:
- Add a `services:` block for PostgreSQL 16 (image: `postgres:16`, env: `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres`, ports: `5432:5432`, health check: `pg_isready`).
- Add a step before tests: `env: TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/tasktoad_test` and run `pnpm --filter api test` then `pnpm --filter web test`.
- Place the test step AFTER typecheck but BEFORE build (tests should gate the build).
- Unit tests (`pnpm --filter web test`) don't need PostgreSQL, so they can run unconditionally.

**2. Task CRUD integration tests** (`apps/api/src/__tests__/task.integration.test.ts`):
Follow the existing pattern in `auth.integration.test.ts` — import from `setup.integration.ts`, use `setupTestDatabase()`/`cleanDatabase()`/`teardownTestDatabase()`.
- **Setup:** Create a user + org + project in `beforeAll` (reuse the signup/createOrg pattern from auth tests, then use `prisma.project.create()`).
- **createTask:** Call the `createTask` resolver directly (import from `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`). Verify task is created in DB with correct title, status='todo', projectId, orgId. Test validation: empty title should throw.
- **updateTask:** Create a task, then update its status to 'in_progress'. Verify DB reflects the change. Test: update title, update priority, update description.
- **deleteTask (archiveTask):** Create a task, archive it via `updateTask` with `archived: true`. Verify `archived` flag is set.
- **bulkUpdateTasks:** Create 3 tasks, bulk update status to 'done'. Verify all 3 updated.

To call resolvers directly, you need a `Context` object. Build it manually:
```typescript
const ctx = { prisma, user, orgId: org.id, loaders: buildLoaders(prisma) };
```
Import `buildLoaders` from `apps/api/src/graphql/loaders.ts`. The resolvers expect `(_parent, args, context)` — pass `{}` as parent.

**3. Sprint integration tests** (`apps/api/src/__tests__/sprint.integration.test.ts`):
- **Setup:** Same as above — user + org + project.
- **createSprint:** Call resolver, verify sprint created with name, projectId, orgId. Verify `isActive` defaults to false.
- **updateSprint:** Activate a sprint (`isActive: true`), verify only one active sprint per project (create a second sprint, activate it, verify the first is deactivated).
- **deleteSprint:** Create a sprint, delete it, verify it's gone from DB.

All integration tests use the `vitest.config.integration.ts` configuration (30s timeout). They will NOT run in CI initially if `TEST_DATABASE_URL` isn't set — that's fine, they'll run when the PostgreSQL service is available.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm --filter api test passes (unit tests)
CI workflow includes PostgreSQL service and pnpm test step
task.integration.test.ts has at least 6 test cases (create, update status/title/priority, archive, bulk update)
sprint.integration.test.ts has at least 4 test cases (create, activate, single-active enforcement, delete)
Integration tests use the existing setup.integration.ts helpers

tasks.title: List virtualization with react-window + position rebalancing
tasks.description:
Add virtual scrolling to long task lists and fractional position rebalancing to prevent position convergence.

**1. Install react-window** — run `pnpm --filter web add react-window && pnpm --filter web add -D @types/react-window`. Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes — add `@types/*` or declare module in a `.d.ts` if type resolution fails.

**2. Virtualize SprintSection task lists** (`apps/web/src/components/SprintSection.tsx`):
BacklogView delegates rendering to SprintSection and BacklogSection. The virtualization should happen inside SprintSection where `TaskRow` elements are mapped.

- Find where tasks are mapped to `<TaskRow>` elements. Wrap with `FixedSizeList` from react-window when the task count exceeds a threshold (e.g., 20 tasks). Below the threshold, render normally (no virtualization overhead for small lists).
- `FixedSizeList` needs a fixed `height` — use `Math.min(tasks.length * ROW_HEIGHT, MAX_LIST_HEIGHT)` where `ROW_HEIGHT = 52` (px, matches TaskRow height) and `MAX_LIST_HEIGHT = 600`.
- Each row renderer receives `{ index, style }` — render `<TaskRow>` with `style` applied to the wrapper div.
- Apply the same pattern in BacklogSection (inside `BacklogView.tsx`) if it separately maps tasks.

**3. Virtualize TableView** (`apps/web/src/components/TableView.tsx`):
TableView renders all tasks as `<tr>` rows in a `<table>`. For virtualization:
- Keep the `<table>` and `<thead>` as-is (static header).
- Replace the `<tbody>` with a `FixedSizeList` that renders `<div>` rows styled with CSS grid to maintain column alignment.
- Each virtual row should match the existing column widths. Use `display: grid; grid-template-columns: <matching widths>` on each row div.
- Set `itemSize={40}` (standard table row height).
- Only virtualize when `tasks.length > 50` — below that, render the standard `<table>` approach.
- Wrap the virtualized section in a container with `overflow: auto` and a max height.

**4. Fractional position rebalancing** (`apps/web/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx`):
The `computePosition` function (lines 21-34) calculates midpoints between adjacent tasks. After many reorders in the same spot, positions converge (e.g., 500, 500.5, 500.25...) losing precision.

Add a `rebalancePositions` check:
```typescript
const MIN_POSITION_GAP = 0.001;

function needsRebalance(sorted: Task[]): boolean {
  for (let i = 1; i < sorted.length; i++) {
    const prev = sorted[i - 1].position ?? (i - 1) * 1000;
    const curr = sorted[i].position ?? i * 1000;
    if (Math.abs(curr - prev) < MIN_POSITION_GAP) return true;
  }
  return false;
}
```
After each reorder in `handleKeyboardReorder`, check `needsRebalance` on the column's sorted tasks. If true, reassign evenly-spaced positions (`(i + 1) * 1000`) to all tasks in the column via `onReorderTask`. This is a background normalization — the user sees the immediate reorder, then positions are silently rebalanced.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes — add @types/react-window or declare module if needed
pnpm lint passes
pnpm build succeeds
react-window installed as dependency
SprintSection virtualizes task lists when > 20 tasks via FixedSizeList
TableView virtualizes rows when > 50 tasks
Small lists render without virtualization (no perf overhead for small projects)
KanbanBoard detects position convergence (gap < 0.001) and rebalances column positions

tasks.title: Dark mode rollout — all views + user toggle with persistence
tasks.description:
Extend dark mode from the current 6 files (Button, Modal, AppLayout, Home, Login, Signup) to ALL remaining views and components. Add a user-facing dark mode toggle.

**Current state:** Tailwind `darkMode: 'class'` is configured. CSS custom properties in `index.css` define dark brand colors under `.dark`. Button and Modal already have `dark:` variants. AppLayout, Home, Login, Signup have dark mode. Everything else (64+ files) does NOT.

**1. Dark mode toggle** (`apps/web/src/pages/AppLayout.tsx`):
Add a dark mode toggle button in the sidebar footer (near the user info area):
```typescript
const [darkMode, setDarkMode] = useState(() => {
  const stored = localStorage.getItem('task-toad-dark-mode');
  if (stored !== null) return stored === 'true';
  return window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
});
useEffect(() => {
  document.documentElement.classList.toggle('dark', darkMode);
  localStorage.setItem('task-toad-dark-mode', String(darkMode));
}, [darkMode]);
```
Render a sun/moon icon button that toggles `darkMode`. Add simple SVG `IconSun`/`IconMoon` to `apps/web/src/components/shared/Icons.tsx` if they don't exist.

**2. Core view components** — add `dark:` variants to backgrounds, text, borders, and interactive elements. The mapping is consistent across all files:
- `bg-white` → add `dark:bg-slate-900`
- `bg-slate-50` → add `dark:bg-slate-800`
- `bg-slate-100` → add `dark:bg-slate-700`
- `text-slate-500/600` → add `dark:text-slate-400`
- `text-slate-700/800/900` → add `dark:text-slate-200/300`
- `border-slate-200/300` → add `dark:border-slate-600/700`
- `hover:bg-slate-50/100` → add `dark:hover:bg-slate-700/800`
- Input fields: add `dark:bg-slate-700 dark:border-slate-600 dark:text-slate-200`

Files to update:
- `ProjectDetail.tsx` (~12 bg-slate patterns)
- `TaskDetailPanel.tsx` (~7 patterns)
- `BacklogView.tsx` (~4 patterns)
- `KanbanBoard.tsx` (~3 patterns)
- `TableView.tsx` (~2 patterns)
- `SprintSection.tsx` (~8 patterns)
- `CommentSection.tsx`, `CalendarView.tsx` (~6), `ProjectDashboard.tsx` (~7), `GanttChart.tsx` (~6)
- `BulkActionBar.tsx` — already dark (`bg-slate-800`), may need minimal adjustment

**3. Shared components:**
- `FilterBar.tsx`: select dropdowns, filter chips
- `SearchInput.tsx`: input background, border, placeholder
- `ToastContainer.tsx`: toast background, text
- `KeyboardShortcutHelp.tsx`: overlay, key badges
- `DependencyBadge.tsx`: badge background
- `MarkdownContent.tsx`: prose text, code blocks, blockquotes
- `MarkdownEditor.tsx`: textarea, preview area
- `Skeleton.tsx`: pulse animations `bg-slate-200` → add `dark:bg-slate-700`

**4. Remaining pages:** `ProfilePage.tsx`, `OrgSettings.tsx` — form inputs, section cards, tables.

Do NOT change semantic colors (status.*, priority.*) or brand colors — those are already handled by CSS custom properties.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
pnpm build succeeds
Dark mode toggle button in AppLayout sidebar with sun/moon icon
Dark mode preference persisted to localStorage and restored on page load
System preference (prefers-color-scheme: dark) used as default when no stored preference
All core views (ProjectDetail, TaskDetailPanel, BacklogView, KanbanBoard, TableView) have dark: variants
All shared components (FilterBar, SearchInput, ToastContainer, Skeleton) have dark: variants
Form inputs across all components have dark: bg/border/text variants
Semantic colors (status, priority) and brand colors are NOT modified
tasks.reviewNotes: MUST REBASE before resubmitting. Run: git rebase main. TableView.tsx has a merge conflict because worker-2 added react-window virtualization (FixedSizeList). After resolving the conflict, make sure dark: variants are applied to BOTH the non-virtualized table path AND the new virtualized CSS grid rows. Then mark task as completed again.

tasks.title: Convert remaining ad-hoc buttons to shared Button + PWA manifest
tasks.description:
Convert the remaining ~26 ad-hoc button elements to use the shared `<Button>` component, and add a PWA manifest for installability.

**1. Button component adoption:**

The shared `<Button>` component (`apps/web/src/components/shared/Button.tsx`) supports variants (primary, secondary, ghost, danger), sizes (sm, md, lg), loading state, and dark mode. ~31 buttons were converted in Wave 13, but ~26 remain.

Search for remaining ad-hoc `<button` elements that use inline Tailwind classes instead of the shared component. Common patterns to find and convert:
- `<button className="...bg-brand-green..."` → `<Button variant="primary">`
- `<button className="...border border-slate-300..."` → `<Button variant="secondary">`
- `<button className="...text-slate-600..."` → `<Button variant="ghost">`
- `<button className="...text-red-600..."` → `<Button variant="danger">`

Key files to check:
- `apps/web/src/pages/Login.tsx` — submit button
- `apps/web/src/pages/Signup.tsx` — submit button
- `apps/web/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx` — submit button
- `apps/web/src/pages/ResetPassword.tsx` — submit button
- `apps/web/src/pages/VerifyEmail.tsx` — resend button
- `apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvite.tsx` — accept button
- `apps/web/src/pages/CreateOrg.tsx` — submit button
- `apps/web/src/pages/NewProject.tsx` — generate/create buttons
- `apps/web/src/components/SprintCreateModal.tsx` — create/cancel buttons
- `apps/web/src/components/SprintPlanModal.tsx` — plan/cancel buttons
- `apps/web/src/components/CloseSprintModal.tsx` — close/cancel buttons
- `apps/web/src/components/TaskPlanApprovalDialog.tsx` — approve/reject/cancel buttons
- `apps/web/src/components/CodePreviewModal.tsx` — create PR/cancel buttons
- `apps/web/src/components/BatchCodeGenModal.tsx` — generate/cancel buttons
- Various cancel buttons across modals (often `text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-800`)

For each conversion:
- Import `Button` from the shared component (adjust path as needed)
- Replace `<button className="..." onClick={...}>Text</button>` with `<Button variant="..." size="..." onClick={...}>Text</Button>`
- If the button has `disabled={loading}` with an inline spinner, use `<Button loading={isLoading}>` instead
- Cancel buttons → `variant="ghost"` or `variant="secondary"`
- Destructive actions (delete, archive) → `variant="danger"`

Do NOT convert icon-only buttons (small action buttons with just an SVG icon and no text) — those have specific sizing that doesn't match the Button component.

**2. PWA manifest** (`apps/web/public/manifest.json`):
Create a web app manifest:
```json
{
  "name": "TaskToad",
  "short_name": "TaskToad",
  "description": "AI-powered project management for teams",
  "start_url": "/",
  "display": "standalone",
  "background_color": "#1B2631",
  "theme_color": "#4CAF50",
  "icons": [
    { "src": "/favicon.png", "sizes": "192x192", "type": "image/png" },
    { "src": "/logo.png", "sizes": "512x512", "type": "image/png" }
  ]
}
```
Link it in `apps/web/index.html`:
```html
<link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.json" />
<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes" />
<meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent" />
```
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm typecheck passes
pnpm lint passes
pnpm build succeeds
At least 20 ad-hoc buttons converted to shared <Button> component
Cancel buttons use variant=ghost or variant=secondary consistently
Destructive buttons use variant=danger
Loading states use Button's loading prop instead of inline spinners
Icon-only buttons are NOT converted (left as-is)
manifest.json created with correct name, icons, theme_color
index.html links manifest.json and has apple-mobile-web-app meta tags
